feature: Phase 7-9: Securities Module, FIX Gateway, AI Bot Expansion, Production Hardening
requirement: Securities Module (equities+bonds, T+2 settlement, CSD), FIX Protocol Gateway (4.4/5.0 broker connectivity), AI Bot Expansion (NLP queries, approval workflow, scheduled tasks), Production Hardening (observability, e2e fixes, cleanup)

tasks.title: Production hardening: fix e2e tests + add settlement cycle route
tasks.description:
Fix remaining e2e test issues and add missing gateway route:
1. Verify the RouteChecker-based 404 fix is working (already wired in main.go:130-131)
2. Add missing POST /api/v1/settlement/cycle route to gateway routes.go — create a handler that proxies to settlement-engine to trigger a new settlement cycle
3. Fix compliance KYC e2e test: the test POSTs to /api/v1/participants and expects JSON object response. Verify the compliance-service handler returns correct format. If the handler returns an array, fix it to return the created participant object.
4. Fix cross-service response format in e2e tests: clearing/netting/margin/settlement list endpoints correctly return arrays. Update e2e tests in lifecycle_test.go to properly handle array responses where appropriate.
5. Remove src/clearing-service/ stub directory (only contains a Dockerfile)
6. Run all e2e tests to verify fixes

Files to check: tests/e2e/e2e_test.go, tests/e2e/lifecycle_test.go, src/gateway/internal/handler/routes.go, src/gateway/internal/handler/handler.go, src/compliance-service/internal/onboarding/

tasks.title: Observability: Prometheus metrics + OpenTelemetry tracing
tasks.description:
Add production-grade observability to the platform:
1. In src/shared/observability/ — add Prometheus metrics registry with standard HTTP request metrics (request_count, request_duration_seconds, request_size_bytes histogram). Export via /metrics endpoint on each service.
2. Add OpenTelemetry trace context propagation: create a TraceMiddleware that extracts/injects W3C trace context headers, creates spans for each request.
3. Add a MetricsServer struct that runs a separate HTTP server on port (service_health_port + 100) for metrics scraping (e.g., matching-engine metrics on 8181).
4. Wire metrics middleware into gateway (already has metrics.MetricsMiddleware stub — enhance it).
5. Add trace_id to structured log output in all services.
6. Write tests for metrics collection and trace propagation.

Keep zero-dependency pattern: implement Prometheus exposition format manually (text/plain with # HELP, # TYPE, metric lines). Do NOT import prometheus/client_golang. For OTel: implement W3C traceparent header parsing manually.

Files: src/shared/observability/metrics.go, src/shared/observability/tracing.go, src/shared/observability/metrics_test.go, src/shared/observability/tracing_test.go, src/gateway/internal/metrics/

tasks.title: Securities module architecture spec
tasks.description:
Write a comprehensive architecture spec for the securities module at docs/securities-architecture.md covering:
1. Asset classes: Equities (common stock, preferred), Bonds (government, corporate), ETFs
2. Instrument model: ISIN, CUSIP, exchange_code, lot_size, tick_size, listing_date, maturity_date (bonds), coupon_rate (bonds), par_value
3. Order types for securities: limit, market, stop, stop-limit with lot size validation and tick size enforcement
4. Short-sale restrictions: locate requirement, uptick rule, restricted list
5. Position limits per security and per participant
6. Securities clearing: CCP (Central Counterparty) model with novation, netting by instrument+settlement_date
7. T+2 settlement state machine: TD (trade date) → TD+1 (affirmation/confirmation/netting) → TD+2 (DVP settlement via CSD)
8. Fail management: failed settlement tracking, buy-in procedures, penalty interest
9. CSD integration interfaces: custody accounts, securities transfer (free-of-payment, delivery-vs-payment), corporate actions (dividends, splits, rights)
10. Database schema design for V26-V28 migrations
11. Gateway API routes: /api/v1/securities/*
12. Kafka event topics for securities lifecycle

This spec will be consumed by T5-T11 implementation tasks. Be specific about data types, field names, and API contracts.

tasks.title: FIX protocol gateway architecture spec
tasks.description:
Write a comprehensive architecture spec for the FIX protocol gateway at docs/fix-gateway-spec.md covering:
1. FIX 4.4 and 5.0 protocol support (FIXT 1.1 transport for 5.0)
2. Supported message types:
   - Session: Logon (A), Logout (5), Heartbeat (0), TestRequest (1), ResendRequest (2), SequenceReset (4), Reject (3)
   - Application: NewOrderSingle (D), OrderCancelRequest (F), OrderCancelReplaceRequest (G), ExecutionReport (8), OrderCancelReject (9)
   - Market Data: MarketDataRequest (V), MarketDataSnapshotFullRefresh (W), MarketDataIncrementalRefresh (X)
3. FIX session management: CompID pairs, sequence number tracking, message store, session schedule
4. FIX-to-REST adapter architecture: FIX message → parse → validate → translate to gateway HTTP API call → translate response back to FIX
5. Broker onboarding: CompID registration, session configuration, password management, IP whitelist
6. Message rate limits per session
7. FIX message logging for regulatory audit trail
8. Service architecture: new Go service at src/fix-gateway/ on port 9098 (FIX acceptor), health on 8089
9. Go implementation approach: use quickfixgo library for protocol handling
10. Database tables for FIX sessions, message store, broker registry
11. Docker Compose integration

tasks.title: Securities DB migrations V26-V28
tasks.description:
Create database migrations for the securities module based on the spec from T3 (read docs/securities-architecture.md first).

V26__securities_instruments.sql:
- CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS securities;
- securities.instruments: id UUID PK, isin VARCHAR(12) UNIQUE, cusip VARCHAR(9), exchange_code VARCHAR(10), name VARCHAR(200), asset_class VARCHAR(20) CHECK (equity/bond/etf), lot_size DECIMAL(18,4), tick_size DECIMAL(18,4), currency VARCHAR(3), listing_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active', par_value DECIMAL(18,4), coupon_rate DECIMAL(8,4), maturity_date DATE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
- securities.restricted_list: instrument_id FK, restriction_type, effective_date, expiry_date, reason
- securities.position_limits: instrument_id FK, participant_id, max_long DECIMAL, max_short DECIMAL, max_notional DECIMAL

V27__securities_trading.sql:
- securities.orders: id UUID PK, isin, participant_id, side, type, quantity, price, settlement_date, short_sale_flag BOOLEAN, locate_id, status, created_at, updated_at
- securities.trades: id UUID PK, buy_order_id FK, sell_order_id FK, instrument_id FK, price DECIMAL(18,4), quantity DECIMAL(18,4), trade_date DATE, settlement_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20), created_at
- securities.positions: participant_id, instrument_id FK, long_qty DECIMAL, short_qty DECIMAL, avg_cost DECIMAL, market_value DECIMAL, unrealized_pnl DECIMAL, updated_at

V28__securities_settlement.sql:
- securities.settlement_instructions: id UUID PK, trade_id FK, delivering_participant, receiving_participant, instrument_id FK, quantity DECIMAL, settlement_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20), csd_reference, fail_reason, created_at, updated_at
- securities.settlement_cycles: id UUID PK, settlement_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20), total_instructions INT, settled_count INT, failed_count INT, started_at, completed_at
- securities.corporate_actions: id UUID PK, instrument_id FK, action_type VARCHAR(20), record_date DATE, ex_date DATE, payment_date DATE, details JSONB, created_at
- securities.custody_accounts: id UUID PK, participant_id, account_number VARCHAR(50), csd_participant_code VARCHAR(20), status VARCHAR(20)

Follow existing migration style from V1-V25. Add appropriate indexes.

tasks.title: Securities matching engine extensions
tasks.description:
Extend the matching engine to support securities trading. Read docs/securities-architecture.md (from T3) and the existing matching engine code at src/matching-engine/.

Add to src/matching-engine/internal/:
1. types/securities.go — SecurityInstrument struct with ISIN, lot_size, tick_size, asset_class, position_limits, short_sale_restricted bool. SecurityOrder extends Order with settlement_date, short_sale_flag, locate_id.
2. orderbook/securities_validator.go — Validate lot size (quantity must be multiple of lot_size), tick size (price must be multiple of tick_size), short-sale restrictions (reject if instrument is restricted and order is short sale without locate), position limits (reject if order would exceed max_long/max_short)
3. engine/securities.go — AddSecurityInstrument(inst SecurityInstrument) to register securities in the engine. SubmitSecurityOrder that runs validators before forwarding to orderbook. Auto-calculate settlement_date as T+2 (skip weekends).
4. Write comprehensive tests: lot size validation, tick size validation, short-sale rejection, position limit enforcement, T+2 date calculation, securities order matching.
5. Target 70%+ coverage on new code.

Do NOT modify existing commodity matching logic — extend via new files. The orderbook itself is asset-class agnostic.

tasks.title: Securities clearing engine extensions
tasks.description:
Extend the clearing engine for securities clearing with CCP model. Read docs/securities-architecture.md (from T3) and existing clearing engine at src/clearing-engine/.

Add to src/clearing-engine/internal/:
1. types/securities.go — SecuritiesTrade, SecuritiesPosition, NettingResult with settlement_date grouping
2. securities/clearing.go — SecuritiesClearingEngine: accept trades, perform novation (CCP as counterparty), calculate net obligations per participant per instrument per settlement_date, generate settlement instructions
3. securities/netting.go — Multi-lateral netting by (instrument, settlement_date). Net quantities and cash amounts across all trades for the same settlement date.
4. securities/position.go — Real-time position tracking: long_qty, short_qty, avg_cost, market_value, unrealized_pnl calculation
5. Write tests: novation creates two CCP legs, netting reduces obligations, position tracking accuracy, settlement instruction generation
6. Target 70%+ coverage.

Do NOT modify existing commodity clearing logic.

tasks.title: T+2 settlement state machine
tasks.description:
Implement the T+2 settlement state machine in the settlement engine. Read docs/securities-architecture.md (from T3) and existing settlement engine at src/settlement-engine/.

Add to src/settlement-engine/internal/:
1. types/securities.go — SettlementInstruction with states: PENDING → AFFIRMED → CONFIRMED → SETTLING → SETTLED | FAILED. SettlementCycle struct.
2. securities/statemachine.go — T+2 lifecycle: TD (receive trade, create PENDING instruction), TD+1 (affirmation/confirmation), TD+2 (DVP settlement). Fail handling with reason tracking.
3. securities/buyin.go — Buy-in procedures for failed settlements: after N days of failure, initiate buy-in at market price + penalty.
4. securities/cycle.go — SettlementCycleManager: RunCycle(date), GetPendingInstructions(date), GetFailedInstructions()
5. Write tests: full lifecycle TD→SETTLED, fail→buy-in, weekend skipping for T+2 calculation, cycle processing
6. Target 70%+ coverage.

Do NOT modify existing commodity settlement (DVP physical delivery) logic.

tasks.title: Securities margin engine extensions
tasks.description:
Extend the margin engine for securities margin calculations. Read docs/securities-architecture.md (from T3) and existing margin engine at src/margin-engine/.

Add to src/margin-engine/internal/:
1. types/securities.go — SecuritiesMarginParams (initial_margin_pct, maintenance_margin_pct, concentration_limit), SecuritiesMarginResult
2. securities/margin.go — SecuritiesMarginCalculator: initial margin (position_value * initial_margin_pct, typically 50%), maintenance margin (typically 25%), concentration risk surcharge, portfolio cross-margining for hedged positions
3. securities/margin_call.go — Margin call workflow: detect equity < maintenance, generate call with deadline (T+3), escalation: warning → call → forced liquidation
4. Write tests: initial margin calc, maintenance check, concentration surcharge, margin call generation, forced liquidation trigger
5. Target 70%+ coverage.

Do NOT modify existing SPAN margin logic for commodities.

tasks.title: Securities gateway routes + handlers
tasks.description:
Add securities API routes to the gateway. Depends on T1 (gateway fixes) and T6 (matching engine securities).

Add to src/gateway/internal/handler/:
1. securities.go — New handler methods: ListSecurities, GetSecurity, SubmitSecurityOrder, GetSecurityPositions, GetSecurityTrades, GetSettlementInstructions, TriggerSettlementCycle, CreateSecurityInstrument (admin), HaltSecurity (admin), ResumeSecurity (admin)
2. Update routes.go — Register all new routes under /api/v1/securities/* with proper auth
3. Add securities public prefixes to auth config for GET methods
4. Write tests for each handler (mock backend responses)
5. Target 60%+ coverage.

Proxy pattern: forward to matching-engine for orders/books, clearing-engine for positions/netting, settlement-engine for settlement.

tasks.title: Securities market-data extensions
tasks.description:
Extend market-data-service for securities market data. Read existing code at src/market-data-service/.

Add to src/market-data-service/internal/:
1. securities/ticker.go — Securities ticker with additional fields: last_price, bid, ask, open, high, low, close, volume, vwap, market_cap, pe_ratio, dividend_yield, 52w_high, 52w_low
2. securities/index.go — Index calculation engine: weighted average of constituent securities prices.
3. securities/corporate_actions.go — Process corporate actions that affect price: stock splits, dividends, rights issues
4. Write tests: ticker update, index calculation, corporate action price adjustment
5. Target 60%+ coverage.

Do NOT modify existing commodity market data logic.

tasks.title: CSD integration interfaces
tasks.description:
Create CSD (Central Securities Depository) integration interfaces in the settlement engine.

Add to src/settlement-engine/internal/csd/:
1. csd.go — CSDClient interface: TransferSecurities, GetBalance, GetCustodyAccount, ProcessCorporateAction
2. mock_csd.go — In-memory mock CSD for testing: maintains balances, validates sufficient balance before transfer, tracks transfer history
3. corporate_actions.go — CorporateActionProcessor: processes dividends, splits, mergers
4. Write tests: transfer success/failure, insufficient balance, corporate action processing
5. Target 80%+ coverage on the mock.

Follows the channel-based Kafka stub pattern — real wire adapters will be swapped in at deployment.

tasks.title: FIX gateway service implementation
tasks.description:
Create the FIX protocol gateway as a new Go service. Read docs/fix-gateway-spec.md (from T4).

Create src/fix-gateway/ as a new Go module:
1. go.mod — module github.com/garudax-platform/fix-gateway, require github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix
2. cmd/fix-gateway/main.go — Service entry point: start FIX acceptor on port 9098, health HTTP on 8089
3. internal/types/ — FIX-specific types: Session, BrokerConfig, OrderTranslation
4. internal/fix/acceptor.go — FIX acceptor using quickfixgo: implement quickfix.Application interface, handle session lifecycle, route incoming messages
5. internal/fix/handlers.go — HandleNewOrderSingle, HandleOrderCancelRequest, HandleMarketDataRequest
6. internal/fix/translator.go — Bidirectional FIX↔REST translation: FIXOrderToREST, RESTTradeToExecutionReport, RESTBookToSnapshot
7. internal/gateway/client.go — HTTP client for gateway API calls
8. internal/store/sessions.go — In-memory session store
9. Write tests: message translation, session lifecycle, order flow, reject handling
10. Target 60%+ coverage.
11. Add Dockerfile

Port convention: FIX acceptor 9098, health HTTP 8089, gRPC 50059.

tasks.title: FIX session management + broker onboarding
tasks.description:
Add FIX session management, broker onboarding, and audit trail. Depends on T13.

Extend src/fix-gateway/internal/:
1. broker/registry.go — BrokerRegistry: RegisterBroker, GetBroker, ValidateSession, UpdateSessionState
2. broker/ratelimit.go — Per-session rate limiter
3. audit/logger.go — FIX message audit trail: LogMessage, QueryMessages (append-only, structured JSON)
4. admin/handlers.go — Admin API: list/get/disconnect sessions, list/register brokers, query audit trail
5. Write tests
6. Target 60%+ coverage.

tasks.title: Securities + FIX admin bot MCP tools
tasks.description:
Add MCP tools for securities and FIX management to the admin bot.

1. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/securities.ts — create_security, halt_security, resume_security, list_securities, get_security_positions, get_settlement_instructions, trigger_settlement_cycle, set_position_limit, manage_restricted_list
2. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/fix.ts — list_fix_sessions, get_fix_session, disconnect_fix_session, register_broker, list_brokers, get_fix_audit
3. Register both tool files in the MCP server's tool registration.
4. Write tests for tool parameter validation and response handling.

Follow the existing tool pattern in src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/.

tasks.title: NLP query engine for admin bot
tasks.description:
Add natural language query capability to the admin bot.

1. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/nlp/intent.ts — Intent classifier: pattern-based matching, keyword extraction, parameter extraction (time range, limit, instrument, participant, status filters)
2. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/nlp/query_builder.ts — Translate intent+params to API calls
3. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/nlp/response_formatter.ts — Format API responses as human-readable text (markdown tables, summary cards)
4. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/query.ts — New MCP tool 'natural_query'
5. Write tests: intent classification accuracy, parameter extraction, query building, response formatting

Pure keyword/regex/rule-based NLP. No ML libraries.

tasks.title: Approval workflow system
tasks.description:
Implement an approval workflow for high-impact admin actions.

1. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/workflow/approval.ts — ApprovalWorkflow: createApprovalRequest, approveRequest, rejectRequest. 15-minute expiry.
2. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/workflow/store.ts — In-memory approval store with history
3. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/approval.ts — MCP tools: propose_action, approve_action, reject_action, list_pending_approvals, get_approval_history
4. Write tests: approval flow, rejection, expiry, concurrent requests

Approval-required actions: create_instrument, halt_instrument, modify_margin_params, modify_circuit_breaker, modify_fee_schedule, mass_cancel, suspend_participant.

tasks.title: Scheduled tasks + alert system for admin bot
tasks.description:
Implement scheduled task automation and configurable alerts.

1. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/scheduler/scheduler.ts — Schedule types: cron, interval, daily (UTC+8 Ulaanbaatar). Built-in: EOD settlement 17:00, daily margin 09:00, weekly compliance Monday 08:00, monthly fees 1st 06:00.
2. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/scheduler/cron.ts — Simple cron expression parser (no external libs)
3. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/alerts/alert_engine.ts — Alert types: margin_breach, circuit_breaker_triggered, large_trade, compliance_violation, system_health, settlement_fail. Periodic evaluation (60s), cooldown.
4. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/scheduler.ts — create_schedule, list_schedules, delete_schedule, get_schedule_history
5. src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/alerts.ts — create_alert, list_alerts, get_active_alerts, acknowledge_alert, delete_alert
6. Write tests: cron parsing, schedule execution, alert evaluation, cooldown logic

tasks.title: Securities trading view in web-ui
tasks.description:
Add securities trading capability to the web-ui React SPA.

1. src/web-ui/src/services/securities.ts — API client
2. src/web-ui/src/pages/SecuritiesTrading.tsx — Securities trading page with instrument selector, order entry form, order book display, recent trades, position summary
3. src/web-ui/src/pages/SecuritiesTrading.module.css
4. Update App.tsx to add route /securities and nav link
5. Export business logic as pure functions: validateLotSize, validateTickSize, formatISIN, calculatePnL
6. Write tests for pure functions and API client

CX ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all must pass):
- Loading state: shows skeleton/spinner while data loads
- Empty state: meaningful message when no data exists
- Error state: user-friendly error message with retry action
- Responsive: works on mobile (375px), tablet (768px), desktop (1280px+)
- Keyboard navigation: all interactive elements reachable via Tab
- Accessible: proper ARIA labels, semantic HTML, color contrast 4.5:1+
- Toast/feedback: user actions produce visible confirmation
- Form validation: inline errors shown on blur, submit disabled until valid
- No raw technical errors visible to user
- Consistent styling: uses project design tokens/theme

5. pages/*.module.css
6. Update sidebar navigation
7. Export pure functions, write tests

CX ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all must pass):
- Loading/empty/error states
- Responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px+
- Keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, semantic HTML
- Toast feedback, form validation
- No raw errors, consistent styling

tasks.title: Securities + FIX e2e integration tests

tests/e2e/securities_test.go: instrument CRUD, order flow, lot/tick size validation, short-sale restriction, positions, settlement cycle, margin calculation.
tests/e2e/fix_test.go: session lifecycle, order submission via FIX, market data request, session rejection.

Use skipIfGatewayUnavailable pattern. Target 15+ test functions.
